data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "The collapse was caused by a systemic failure of the palatial redistribution economy due to seismic disruption of trade routes and agricultural surplus.", "mechanism": "Cascading resource starvation triggered by earthquake damage to port infrastructure and grain stores, breaking the feedback loop between central authority and peripheral producers.", "scope": {"covers": ["Mycenaean Greece", "Ugarit", "Hittite Anatolia", "Levantine city-states"], "excludes": ["Egypt"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Archaeological evidence showing intact grain silos and port facilities in Ugarit immediately prior to destruction layers.", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}], "prose_notes": "This is the modal 'Systems Collapse' theory, attributing the event to fragility in interconnected supply chains exacerbated by natural disaster."}
